name: A curated list of quality Lua packages and resources.
description: A curated list of quality Lua packages and resources.

links.title: Lua
links.description: Lua's original ANSI C interpreter.

links.title: Lua Repo
links.description: The official Lua repo, as seen by the Lua team, mirrored to GitHub.

links.title: LuaJIT
links.description: High-performance Just-In-Time compiler for Lua.

links.title: LLVM-Lua
links.description: Compiles Lua to LLVM.

links.title: lua.vm.js
links.description: Lua VM on the web; a direct port of the C interpreter via LLVM, emscripten, and asm.js.

links.title: Moonshine
links.description: A Lua VM implemented in JavaScript. Slower than lua.vm.js, but with better docs, examples, and JS interfacing.

links.title: Fengari
links.description: The Lua VM rewritten in Javascript with seamless JS and DOM interoperability.

links.title: MoonSharp
links.description: A Lua interpreter written entirely in C# for the .NET, Mono and Unity platforms.

links.title: UniLua
links.description: A pure C# implementation of Lua 5.2, focused on compatibility with the Unity game engine.

links.title: lupa
links.description: Python bindings to LuaJIT2.

links.title: LuaRocks
links.description: De-facto tool for installing Lua modules as packages called "rocks", plus public rock repository and website.  Much like npm or pip.

links.title: Lake
links.description: A build engine written in Lua, similar to Ruby's rake.

links.title: Luabuild
links.description: Highly customizable Lua 5.2 build system.

links.title: luastatic
links.description: Simple tool for turning Lua programs into standalone executables.

links.title: omnia
links.description: A batteries-included creator of standalone executables, built on top of luastatic.

links.title: ProFi
links.description: Simple profiler that works with LuaJIT and produces a report file.

links.title: luatrace
links.description: Toolset for tracing/analyzing/profiling script execution and generating detailed reports.

links.title: StackTracePlus
links.description: Drop-in upgrade to Lua's stack traces which adds local context and improves readability.

links.title: MobDebug
links.description: Powerful remote debugger with breakpoints and stack inspection. Used by ZeroBraneStudio.

links.title: lovebird
links.description: Browser-based debug console. Originally made for LÖVE, but works in any project with LuaSocket support.

links.title: Lua Development Tools
links.description: Eclipse plugin which provides code completion, debugging, and more. Built on Metalua.

links.title: Lua for IDEA
links.description: IntelliJ IDEA plugin which, among other things, provides code completion, smart highlighting, and experimental debugging.

links.title: ZeroBraneStudio
links.description: Lightweight, customizable, cross-platform Lua-dedicated IDE with code completion and analysis, written in Lua. Has broad debugging support for numerous Lua engines.

links.title: BabeLua
links.description: Lua editor/debugger extension for VS2012-13 with highlighting, auto-completion, linting, and formatting capabilities.

links.title: lua-mode
links.description: Emacs major mode for editing Lua.

links.title: vscode-lua
links.description: VSCode intellisense and linting.

links.title: Lua Fun
links.description: High-performance functional programming library designed for LuaJIT.

links.title: Moses
links.description: Functional programming utility belt, inspired by Underscore.js.

links.title: Penlight
links.description: Broad, heavyweight utility library, inspired by Python's standard libs. Provides the batteries that Lua doesn't.

links.title: lua-stdlib
links.description: Middle-weight standard library extension; adds some useful data structures, utility functions, and basic functional stuff.

links.title: Microlight
links.description: A little library of useful Lua functions; the 'extra light' version of Penlight.

links.title: compat53
links.description: Compatibility module providing Lua-5.3-style APIs for Lua 5.2 and 5.1.

links.title: RxLua
links.description: Reactive Extensions, Observables, etc.

links.title: LÖVE 2D
links.description: Desktop game development platform. Cross-platform, feature-complete, well-adopted.

links.title: Corona SDK
links.description: Development platform for iOS and Android. Proprietary, but used by numerous top games and apps, totaling over 150 million downloads.

links.title: MOAI
links.description: Open source, cross-platform, mobile game development framework. Minimalist C++ engine powered by Lua scripting.

links.title: Drystal
links.description: Open source, games can run on Linux or on any platform with a recent web browser.

links.title: Amulet
links.description: Open source, audio/visual toolkit suitable for small games and experimentation. It runs on Windows, Mac, Linux, HTML5 and iOS.

links.title: LÖVR
links.description: 3D framework for creating virtual reality experiences, inspired by LÖVE 2D.

links.title: Coronium
links.description: Simple cloud platform supporting analytics, data objects, user management, and more.

links.title: lurker
links.description: Shortens the iteration cycle by auto-swapping changed Lua files in a running LÖVE project.

links.title: HUMP
links.description: A set of lightweight helpers for LÖVE; a game-oriented utility belt.

links.title: moaifiddle
links.description: Edit and share short scripts for the MOAI game engine and run them in the browser using WebGL.

links.title: Jumper
links.description: Fast, lightweight, and easy-to-use pathfinding library for grid-based games.

links.title: lume
links.description: Utility belt library geared toward game development.

links.title: NoobHub
links.description: Network multiplayer for Corona, LÖVE, and more, following a simple pub-sub model.

links.title: bump.lua
links.description: Minimal rectangle-based collision detection which handles tunnelling and basic collision resolution.

links.title: HardonCollider
links.description: Detect collisions between arbitrarily positioned and rotated shapes of any type.

links.title: flux
links.description: A fast, lightweight tweening library for Lua with easing functions and the ability to group tweens together.

links.title: tween.lua
links.description: Small library for tweening, with several easing functions.

links.title: termtris
links.description: A tetris clone, written in literate style with "an emphasis on learn-from-ability".

links.title: PacPac
links.description: A Pac-man clone, made with LÖVE.

links.title: Mari0
links.description: Fusion of Mario and Portal, made with LÖVE. See also its [wikipedia entry](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mari0).

links.title: Journey to the Center of Hawkthorne
links.description: 2D platformer based on Community's [Digital Estate Planning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_Estate_Planning) episode, made with LÖVE.

links.title: lua-log
links.description: Asynchronous logging library with pluggable writers for file system, network, ZeroMQ, and more.

links.title: LuaLogging
links.description: Log4j-inspired logging library supporting various appenders.

links.title: luasyslog
links.description: Log to syslog, based on LuaLogging.

links.title: OpenResty
links.description: A fast and scalable web application platform created by extending Nginx with Lua. Today's de-facto Lua web platform, used heavily by Cloudflare, Taobao, Tencent, and others.

links.title: turbo
links.description: Event-driven, non-blocking, LuaJIT-based networking suite and framework, inspired by Tornado.

links.title: Kepler Project
links.description: A collection of web-oriented projects using a common set of standards and components.

links.title: Pegasus.lua
links.description: Pegasus.lua is a http server to work with web applications written in Lua language.

links.title: awesome-resty
links.description: A list like this one, but focused on OpenResty.

links.title: ngx_lua
links.description: The core piece of OpenResty. Embeds Lua in Nginx and exposes, among other things, the cosocket API for non-blocking sockets (compatible with LuaSocket's API).

links.title: OpenResty GitHub Organization
links.description: Home of the repositories for ngx_lua, ngx_openresty, and many related modules.

links.title: lua-resty-http
links.description: Lua HTTP client driver, built on the cosocket API.

links.title: Lapis
links.description: Full-stack framework for Lua and OpenResty. Like the Django or Rails of Lua. Supports Moonscript.

links.title: ledge
links.description: Lua module providing scriptable, RFC-compliant HTTP cache functionality.

links.title: Sailor
links.description: An MVC web framework compatible with OpenResty, Apache and other webservers.

links.title: Kong
links.description: Microservice & API Management Layer.

links.title: ansicolors
links.description: Simple function for printing to the console in color.

links.title: cliargs
links.description: A simple command-line argument parsing module.

links.title: lua-term
links.description: Terminal operations and manipulations.

links.title: argparse
links.description: A feature-rich command line parser inspired by argparse for Python.

links.title: Lumen
links.description: Simple concurrent task scheduling.

links.title: ConcurrentLua
links.description: Implements an Erlang-style message-passing concurrency model.

links.title: cqueues
links.description: Library for managing sockets, signals, and threads based on an event loop with coroutines.

links.title: llthreads
links.description: A simple wrapper for low-level pthreads & WIN32 threads.

links.title: llthreads2
links.description: Newer rewrite of llthreads.

links.title: lanes
links.description: Library implementing a message passing model with one OS thread per Lua thread.

links.title: luaproc
links.description: Message-passing model which allows multiple threads per OS thread and easily generalizes across a network. See also [the paper](http://www.inf.puc-rio.br/~roberto/docs/ry08-05.pdf) where it originated.

links.title: lustache
links.description: Mustache template implementation.

links.title: etlua
links.description: Embedded Lua templates, ERB-style.

links.title: lua-resty-template
links.description: Lua-oriented template engine for OpenResty, somewhat Jinja-like.

links.title: LDoc
links.description: Documentation generator which modernizes and extends [LuaDoc](http://keplerproject.github.io/luadoc/).

links.title: Locco
links.description: Lua port of [Docco](http://ashkenas.com/docco/), the "quick-and-dirty, hundred-line-long, literate-programming-style documentation generator".

links.title: docroc
links.description: Parse comments into a Lua table to generate documentation.

links.title: 30log
links.description: Minimalist OOP library with basic classes, inheritance, and mixins in 30 lines.

links.title: middleclass
links.description: Simple but robust OOP library with inheritance, methods, metamethods, class variables and mixins.

links.title: LuaFileSystem
links.description: Extends and complements Lua's built-in set of file system functions.

links.title: luaposix
links.description: Bindings for POSIX APIs, including curses.

links.title: lunix
links.description: Bindings to common Unix system APIs, striving for thread-safety.

links.title: lua-path
links.description: File system path manipulation library.

links.title: LuaDate
links.description: Date and time module with parsing, formatting, addition/subtraction, localization, and ISO 8601 support.

links.title: cron.lua
links.description: Time-related functions for Lua, inspired by JavaScript's setTimeout and setInterval.

links.title: luatx
links.description: Time, date, and timezone library.

links.title: magick
links.description: Lua bindings to ImageMagick for LuaJIT using FFI.

links.title: LuaFFT
links.description: An easy to use Fast Fourier Transformation package in pure Lua.

links.title: Worp
links.description: Sound/music/DSP engine written for LuaJIT.

links.title: eLua
links.description: Lua, extended with optimizations and specific features for efficient and portable embedded software development.

links.title: SciLua
links.description: Numerical/scientific computing framework built on LuaJIT, with an interface to R.

links.title: Torch7
links.description: Scientific computing framework with wide support for machine learning algorithms, used by Facebook, Google, and more.

links.title: lhf's Lua Tools
links.description: Assorted libraries and tools, many math- or data-related.

links.title: lua-cjson
links.description: Blazing fast JSON encoding/decoding implemented in C and exposed to Lua.

links.title: luajson
links.description: JSON encoder/decoder implemented in Lua on top of LPeg.

links.title: dkjson
links.description: JSON encoder/decoder implemented in pure Lua.

links.title: json.lua
links.description: A fast and tiny JSON library in pure Lua.

links.title: LuaExpat
links.description: SAX XML parser via binding to the Expat library.

links.title: SLAXML
links.description: Pure Lua SAX-like streaming XML parser.

links.title: lua-MessagePack
links.description: Pure Lua implementation of MessagePack.

links.title: lua-cmsgpack
links.description: A MessagePack C implementation with Lua bindings, as used by Redis.=

links.title: LPeg
links.description: A pattern-matching library for Lua, based on Parsing Expression Grammars.

links.title: lpeg_patterns
links.description: A collection of LPeg patterns.

links.title: LuLPeg
links.description: A pure Lua implementation of LPeg v0.12.

links.title: LPegLJ
links.description: A pure LuaJIT implementation of LPeg v1.0.

links.title: LPegLabel
links.description: An extension of LPeg adding support for labeled failures.

links.title: lyaml
links.description: YAML encoding/decoding via binding to LibYAML.

links.title: lunamark
links.description: Converts Markdown to other textual formats including HTML and LaTeX. Uses LPeg for fast parsing.

links.title: LXSH
links.description: A collection of lexers and syntax highlighters written with LPeg.

links.title: lua-pb
links.description: Protocol Buffers implementation.

links.title: i18n.lua
links.description: Internationalization library with locales, formatting, and pluralization.

links.title: inspect.lua
links.description: Human-readable representation of Lua tables.

links.title: serpent
links.description: Serializer and pretty printer.

links.title: Ser
links.description: Dead simple serializer with good performance.

links.title: say
links.description: Simple string key-value store for i18n.

links.title: lua-zlib
links.description: Simple streaming interface to zlib for gzip/gunzip.

links.title: lua-zip
links.description: Lua binding to libzip. Reads and writes zip files.

links.title: LuaCrypto
links.description: Lua bindings to OpenSSL.

links.title: lua-lockbox
links.description: A collection of cryptographic primitives written in pure Lua.

links.title: luatweetnacl
links.description: Bindings to tweetnacl, modern high-security cryptographic library.

links.title: luaossl
links.description: "Most comprehensive OpenSSL module in the Lua universe" - used by lapis, kong, and lua-http.

links.title: LuaSocket
links.description: Networking extension which provides a socket API for TCP and UDP, and implements HTTP, FTP, and SMTP.

links.title: lua-websockets
links.description: WebSocket client and server modules. Webserver-agnostic, implemented in Lua on top of LuaSocket.

links.title: lua-cURLv3
links.description: Lua binding to libcurl.

links.title: lua-http
links.description: Asynchronous HTTP and WebSocket library with client and server APIs, TLS, and HTTP/2; based on cqueues.

links.title: LuaSQL
links.description: Simple interface for connecting to ODBC, ADO, Oracle, MySQL, SQLite and PostgreSQL.

links.title: pgmoon
links.description: Lua PostgreSQL driver for OpenResty, LuaSocket, and cqueues.

links.title: lua-resty-mysql
links.description: Lua MySQL driver for OpenResty.

links.title: lua-resty-cassandra
links.description: Lua Cassandra client driver for OpenResty and others.

links.title: redis-lua
links.description: Pure Lua client library for Redis.

links.title: lua-resty-redis
links.description: Lua Redis client driver for OpenResty.

links.title: lredis
links.description: Asynchronous Redis client with pipelining and Pub/Sub support; based on cqueues.

links.title: lua-zmq
links.description: Lua bindings to ZeroMQ.

links.title: lzmq
links.description: A newer Lua binding to ZeroMQ.

links.title: lua-resty-kafka
links.description: Kafka client driver based on OpenResty cosockets.

links.title: lua-resty-rabbitmqstomp
links.description: RabbitMQ client library based on OpenResty cosockets.

links.title: busted
links.description: BDD-style unit testing framework with great docs and Moonscript support.

links.title: telescope
links.description: Flexible and highly customizable testing library.

links.title: luassert
links.description: Assertion library extending Lua's built-in assertions.

links.title: lust
links.description: Minimal test framework.

links.title: LuaJIT FFI
links.description: LuaJIT's mechanism for calling external C functions and using C data structures from pure Lua code.

links.title: luaffi
links.description: Standalone FFI library, compatible with the LuaJIT FFI interface.

links.title: luadec51
links.description: Lua Decompiler for Lua version 5.1.

links.title: luacov
links.description: Simple coverage analyzer, used by busted and telescope for checking test coverage.

links.title: luacov-coveralls
links.description: LuaCov reporter for coveralls.io.

links.title: luacheck
links.description: Simple static analyzer which detects accidental globals and undefined or shadowed locals.

links.title: Metalua
links.description: Pure Lua parser and compiler, used for generating ASTs. A number of other tools make use of the Metalua parser in this way.

links.title: LuaInspect
links.description: Lua's most powerful code analysis and linting tool, built on Metalua. Used by ZeroBraneStudio, among others.

links.title: LuaMinify
links.description: Minifier which also brings its own static analysis tools, lexer, and parser.

links.title: Typed Lua
links.description: A typed superset of Lua that compiles to plain Lua.

links.title: lua-parser
links.description: A Lua 5.3 parser written using LPegLabel, with improved error messages.

links.title: punchdrunk.js
links.description: Moonshine + LÖVE API reimplementation = run LÖVE games in the browser.

links.title: luvit
links.description: Node.js's underlying architecture (libUV) with Lua on top instead of JavaScript.

links.title: graphql-lua
links.description: Lua implementation of [GraphQL](http://graphql.org/).

links.title: luakit
links.description: Fast, small, webkit based browser framework extensible by Lua.

links.title: Hammerspoon
links.description: A powerful, extensible OS X automation tool. A community-maintained fork of [Mjolnir](http://www.mjolnir.io/).

links.title: kpie
links.description: A scripting utility to juggle windows.

links.title: lumail
links.description: A console-based mail client, with extensive scripting capabilities.

links.title: AwesomeWM
links.description: A highly configurable and extensible window manager for X, scripted and configured by Lua.

links.title: Textadept
links.description: Extremely lightweight, customizable, cross-platform editor, written (mostly) in (and scripted by) Lua.

links.title: KoReader
links.description: An ebook reader application supports PDF, DJVU, EPUB, FB2 and much more, running on Kindle, Kobo, PocketBook and Android devices.

links.title: MoonScript
links.description: Moonscript is a dynamic scripting language that compiles to Lua. It reduces verbosity and provides a rich set of features like comprehensions and classes. Its author calls it 'CoffeeScript for Lua'.

links.title: sitegen
links.description: A static site generator which uses MoonScript and supports HTML and Markdown, page grouping, and plugins.

links.title: lua-l
links.description: The official Lua mailing list, and one of the focal points of the Lua community.

links.title: Lua.Space
links.description: The Lua community blog.

links.title: Lua Users Foundation
links.description: An association of individuals with the mission of supporting and promoting Lua and its community and ecosystems.

links.title: lua-users.org
links.description: A site for and by users of Lua, featuring an IRC channel, a web archive of lua-l, and a large wiki.

links.title: Lua Workshop
links.description: Annual 2-day meeting of the Lua community, in rotating locations.

links.title: Lua Conf
links.description: Annual 1-day Lua conference in Brazil.

links.title: FOSDEM
links.description: Annual 2-day gathering of F/OSS developers in Brussels which sometimes has a "Lua devroom".

links.title: Lua 5.3 Glossary
links.description: A glossary of some essential Lua terms.

links.title: Lua-users style guide
links.description: A general, high-level style guide; unopinionated, easily agreed on.

links.title: Olivine style guide
links.description: A more opinionated and specific, and therefore more rigorous, guide.

links.title: Lua Crash Course
links.description: Short crash course readover, or reference for when you forget the basics.

links.title: Learn Lua in 15 Minutes
links.description: A well-commented example file which covers the basics.

links.title: Learning Lua from JS
links.description: An overview of the similarities and differences between Lua and JS; a great start for JavaScript folks looking to pick up Lua.

links.title: lua-users tutorial
links.description: In-depth collection of tutorials aimed at newcomers.

links.title: Lua Missions
links.description: A series of 'Missions' to work through which are designed to teach aspects of Lua along the way.

links.title: Creating an Image Server
links.description: Walks through setting up and using OpenResty to build a simple image processing server; a great starting point for playing with OpenResty.

links.title: Embedding Lua in C
links.description: An introductory walkthrough of embedding Lua in a C program. A bit dated, but still a great walkthrough.

links.title: Lua: Good, bad, and ugly parts
links.description: A thorough summary of the good, different, bad, and ugly aspects of Lua, including many subtle quirks, by the author of ZeroBraneStudio.

links.title: Lua states, libraries, coroutines and memory
links.description: Diagrams and explains some more advanced concepts of the Lua VM, particularly when interfacing with C.

links.title: Roberto's Talks
links.description: History of talks given by Lua's chief architect, with slides for each.

links.title: Lua Workshop Talks
links.description: High-quality talks are given at each ~annual Lua Workshop, and a history of them is online, slides included.

links.title: Programming in Lua
links.description: The authoritative intro to all aspects of Lua programming, written by Lua's chief architect. Three editions released; first edition available online.

links.title: Lua Quick Reference
links.description: A quick reference on how to program in and embed Lua 5.1 through 5.3, by the creator of Textadept.

links.title: Programming Gems
links.description: A collection of articles covering existing wisdom and practices on programming well in Lua, in a broad variety of use cases.

links.title: Lua Programming
links.description: A shorter overview of the language, up to date for Lua 5.2, and available online.

links.title: Where Lua is Used
links.description: A comprehensive list of stand-alone programs written in or extensible using Lua.
